candidate_problems.text: How can 'load-bearing' be made into a non-circular, operational criterion that distinguishes genuinely indispensable explanatory parts from merely convenient descriptions? The conjecture relies on counterfactual fragility and non-substitutability, but without a principled way to individuate parts, levels of abstraction, and explanatory outcomes, almost any explanation can be redescribed as either fragile or robust.

What is the relation between explanatory load-bearingness and truth-seeking versus audience-relative pedagogy? A component may be indispensable for deriving or generating a result, yet dispensable for understanding, communication, or prediction. The open question is whether the conjecture tracks objective explanatory structure or only a representation-dependent feature of how explanations are packaged for agents.

When does apparent fragility indicate real irreducible complementarity, and when is it just a symptom of a bad model that has failed to compress to a deeper invariant? The conjecture raises a major tension between explanations that are truly conjunctive at the right level and explanations that only seem all load-bearing because hidden redundancy or a better abstraction has not yet been found.
